Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    3 Drive Electric    This Driver Handbook represents the terms and conditions  of the provision of cars through Drive Electric - Barclays Car  Leasing Scheme (“Drive Electric” or “Scheme”). Drive Electric is  administered by Ayvens on behalf of Barclays. Car benefits and the operation of Drive Electric are entirely at the  discretion of Barclays. Barclays reserves the right to discontinue,  suspend, vary or withdraw the Scheme or any part thereof at any  time. Additionally, Barclays reserves the right to remove the benefit  from individual employees that fail to observe the terms and  conditions, or the spirit of the Scheme. This Driver Handbook will be reviewed on a regular basis. It is  your responsibility to read the driver handbook from time to  time and to understand the terms and conditions of the Scheme.

It is  your responsibility to read the driver handbook from time to  time and to understand the terms and conditions of the Scheme. The Drive Electric website, administered by Ayvens, provides  you with access to order new Electric Vehicles (EVs) of Plug-in  Hybrid Electric Vehicles (PHEVs) in exchange for agreeing a fixed  monthly reduction in salary. The provision of the vehicle includes  all services required to run your car (save as set out in this driver  handbook, for example fuel, including electricity for charging, and  oil), providing you with certainty of car related costs for up to the  next four years. If you elect to join Drive Electric, you are required to comply  with the provisions of this Driver Handbook and agree to the  variation of your terms and conditions of employment.

If you elect to join Drive Electric, you are required to comply  with the provisions of this Driver Handbook and agree to the  variation of your terms and conditions of employment. The  order confirmation on Drive Electric and the My Rewards website  is your acceptance of the Driver Handbook, the terms and  conditions of participation within Drive Electric and the change  to your gross salary.

The  order confirmation on Drive Electric and the My Rewards website  is your acceptance of the Driver Handbook, the terms and  conditions of participation within Drive Electric and the change  to your gross salary. Introduction Specifically, your agreement to join includes: •	 Confirmation of a reduced salary by a certain value and for  an agreed time period •	 Your understanding that this takes effect as a variation to  your contract of employment •	 Your agreement that you have fully understood the Terms  and Conditions of participation contained herein and your  agreement to be bound by them •	 Confirmation that you (and any named driver) meet the  eligibility criteria to join the Scheme (as set out in this Driver  Handbook) and that the information provided to the insurer  is complete and accurate •	 Agreement to provide the necessary personal information  and mandates as required •	 Agreement that Barclays/Ayvens is entitled to use your  personal information (including sensitive information) in  connection with the management and administration of the  Scheme (and for no other purpose) •	 Authorisation for Barclays to deduct certain costs from  your net pay through payroll as detaile

4      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Personal Details To ensure that information relating to your participation in Drive  Electric is up to date and timely it is vital that you maintain your  personal contact details via MyHR/Personal actions/Personal  details. This information is provided to Ayvens on a regular basis. Your personal details (i.e. home/work address, phone numbers  and e-mail addresses) may also be provided to other third party  companies in relation to the on-going management of your  vehicle. All information will be securely stored and not used for unrelated  matters and provision of the information is a condition of use of  the vehicle. Introduction What is this Scheme? Drive Electric is an employee benefit provided via My Rewards. An Electric Vehicle (EV) or Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV)  of your choice is provided by Barclays for your business and  personal use under a salary sacrifice arrangement.

An Electric Vehicle (EV) or Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle (PHEV)  of your choice is provided by Barclays for your business and  personal use under a salary sacrifice arrangement. Due to the increased taxation of company cars and the  implication on increased costs to both Barclays and Barclays  colleagues, Drive Electric does not offer access to Petrol, Diesel  or other Hybrid Vehicles. Drive Electric provides you with access to the use of a brand new,  fully managed, maintained and insured company car at a level of  net cost that may not be available outside of the Scheme. What are the benefits of the Scheme? •	 A brand new, fully managed, maintained and insured car for  2, 3 or 4 year periods •	 Wide choice of make, model, optional extras, colour and trim  levels •	 No deposit, credit checks or personal finance agreements  required •	 An easy to use online portal for quoting and ordering  vehicles •	 Hassle-free motoring as all services are accessed via Ayvens.

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    5 What does the Scheme include? The reduction in salary is fixed for the duration of the salary  sacrifice period which matches the underlying vehicle lease  between Barclays and Ayvens.

It covers the cost to Barclays of  providing the vehicle to you comprised of and including the  following: •	 The lease cost of the vehicle •	 Road tax •	 Maintenance and servicing costs, in line with manufacturer  recommendations •	 No quibble replacement tyres including accidental damage  and punctures •	 Fully comprehensive Class 1 insurance fixed for the period  •	 Business use is included for all drivers on the policy •	 Accident Management •	 5-star Breakdown Assistance/Recovery •	 28 day relief vehicle should your car be immobilised by  mechanical breakdown •	 Early Termination Cover (conditions apply – see section on  early terminations) •	 Driving licence check •	 MOT •	 End of Contract damage cover (conditions apply – see  section on End of Contract) Introduction What does the Scheme not include? ✖	 All fuel costs,  including any electricity/power, topping up oil  used between services, windscreen wash additives, refill or  top-up of AdBlue, cleaning and valeting ✖	 C

6      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 What is salary sacrifice? Salary sacrifice is a mechanism whereby you contractually agree  to give up part of your gross salary and in return receive a non- cash benefit provided by Barclays (in this case, a company car). This means in effect that the cost to the company of providing  you with a company car is offset by a reduction to your gross  salary before income tax and NIC are deducted. Who is the salary sacrifice  agreement between? The salary sacrifice agreement is between you and Barclays and  is a variation to your contract of employment. The vehicle is  owned by Ayvens and leased to Barclays who in turn provide it to  you as a company car under the Scheme. Is there a maximum level of salary  that I can sacrifice? This scheme represents just one of the options available  within the wider Barclays My Rewards voluntary employee  benefits scheme, which also operate using the salary sacrifice  mechanism.

Is there a maximum level of salary  that I can sacrifice? This scheme represents just one of the options available  within the wider Barclays My Rewards voluntary employee  benefits scheme, which also operate using the salary sacrifice  mechanism. Your combined salary sacrifice for all benefits  cannot reduce your normal salary to a level below the National  Minimum Wage (“NMW”), National Living Wage (NLW) or an  appropriate statutory payment level. However, Barclays also imposes a restriction to prevent you from  sacrificing more than 75% of your base gross salary, a restriction  which already exists for the My Rewards salary sacrifice benefits. All of these checks are performed when you select the benefits  within the My Rewards platform. Contact details You will find all of the important contact details you need at the  end of this document.

Contact details You will find all of the important contact details you need at the  end of this document. The most important number you will need  is: Ayvens Driverline - 0344 371 2455 Salary sacrifice and tax What is the effect of salary sacrifice  on my tax? Your income tax and national insurance contributions will be  reduced as your gross salary will be reduced by this salary  sacrifice agreement. Instead, cars provided under this Scheme are company cars and  you will pay tax on the benefit of the company car through payroll. As Drive Electric only offers EVs and PHEVs, tax is based on the car’s  specific “Benefit in Kind” (BIK) value, known as the taxable value or  P11d value which is 2% for the 2022/23 tax year, increasing to 5% for  the 2027/28 tax year (see page 9 for further details).

As Drive Electric only offers EVs and PHEVs, tax is based on the car’s  specific “Benefit in Kind” (BIK) value, known as the taxable value or  P11d value which is 2% for the 2022/23 tax year, increasing to 5% for  the 2027/28 tax year (see page 9 for further details). If you ordered a car before 1 October 2021, with CO2 above 75g/  km, the tax calculation will be different for you and you will have  been advised of the indicative tax position when you received your  order confirmation. Due to the low Government taxation of EVs/PHEVs you should be  able to make savings versus the normal retail cost of these vehicles. How is the BIK tax for the car calculated? The taxable value of the car is based on the following factors -  the “list price” of the car, its level of CO2 emissions, its electric  range, and fuel type.

How is the BIK tax for the car calculated? The taxable value of the car is based on the following factors -  the “list price” of the car, its level of CO2 emissions, its electric  range, and fuel type. The “list price” of the car is defined as the manufacturer’s  published VAT inclusive list price on the day of first registration,  including delivery charges and any optional extras fitted (also  VAT inclusive). It is also often referred to as the P11D price. CO2 emissions levels are published by the manufacturer and  reflects the grams of carbon dioxide emitted by the car for each  kilometre travelled. CO2 emissions will be 0g/km for an EV and  between 1-50g/km for a PHEV. The emissions figure maps to a “relevant percentage” according  to graduated tables published by HMRC (see below), which  include rates up to and including the 2027/28 tax year.

The emissions figure maps to a “relevant percentage” according  to graduated tables published by HMRC (see below), which  include rates up to and including the 2027/28 tax year. The  lower the CO2 emissions and longer electric range, the lower the  relevant percentage and ultimately the lower the BIK charge on  which tax is due at your marginal income tax rate..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    7 Salary sacrifice and tax Electric Vehicle (EV) – e.g. Volkswagen ID.3 Example correct as at 17 November 2022 Taxable List Price (P11D) – £32,415	 Fuel Type – Electric	  CO2  – 0 g/km	 Electric Range – 263 miles  Petrol Plug-in Hybrid Vehicle (PHEV) – e.g.

8      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Salary sacrifice and tax The net (post tax) cost is made up of the salary sacrificed, less  the tax and NIC you would have paid on that salary, plus the  income tax due on the BIK tax due on the vehicle. Where you  use the “net pay modeller” in My Rewards to assess your net  pay alongside your other selected benefits, the tax due in the  current tax year will be used. Please note, however, that you will  be required to pay the correct amount of tax due based on the  appropriate calculation method, depending on your choice of  car, for each tax year. The table on the following page illustrates  the changes to the BIK up to April 2028. What is my marginal income tax rate? The rate at which you pay tax depends upon your personal  circumstances and your total annual gross taxable income,  which can include income from the following: earnings from  Barclays (e.g.

What is my marginal income tax rate? The rate at which you pay tax depends upon your personal  circumstances and your total annual gross taxable income,  which can include income from the following: earnings from  Barclays (e.g. basic salary, bonus payments, share incentive  income, and other cash sums) added to the sum of the taxable  values of your selected benefits (such as a car through this  Scheme), and can also include any other employment income  you receive as well as other qualifying income. In previous years a single, common set of Income Tax rules were  applied in the UK. Since April 2017 a different set of thresholds  applies if you live in Scotland where Income Tax is paid to the  Scottish Government. The thresholds between rate bands are set every year by the UK  Government or, if living in Scotland, the Scottish Government,  as are the personal allowance levels (the 0% rate band that  individuals enjoy).

The thresholds between rate bands are set every year by the UK  Government or, if living in Scotland, the Scottish Government,  as are the personal allowance levels (the 0% rate band that  individuals enjoy). Your marginal income tax rate can be  deduced by summing your earnings and the total taxable value  of your selected benefits, removing the personal allowance from  that total and applying the relevant bandings to what is left. Please do note that this could be more complex if your taxable  remuneration is near the thresholds. Further information can be found at:  https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/rates-and- allowances-income-tax/income-tax-rates-and-allowances- current-and-past.

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    9 Salary sacrifice and tax How will I be taxed on the car benefit? When the car is delivered to you and the salary sacrifice period  commences, Ayvens will advise the Barclays payroll team of  the correct taxable value for the car each month and, in normal  circumstances, you will be taxed on that value through payroll. As  the benefit is being taxed via payroll, a taxable value relating to  the car will also be displayed in the ‘My Rewards Taxable Benefits’  section of your payslip. If you have other taxable benefits, this value  will represent the total taxable value of all of your benefits. There is  no further action required on your part to notify HMRC of the receipt  of the car benefit.

There is  no further action required on your part to notify HMRC of the receipt  of the car benefit. There may, in certain circumstances (see further information  below), be a requirement for Barclays to complete a P11D form to  report the taxable value relating to your car where it has not been  possible to tax this benefit via payroll. This will generally be if you  do not have enough net pay in a particular tax year from which to  collect the tax relating to your car. If a P11D is required, the P11D  will report the taxable value relating to your car. If you are in receipt  of a P11D, this will be submitted by Barclays to HMRC by 6th July  following the end of the tax year and you should also receive a copy  of the form. You will then pay tax either through self-assessment  or HMRC may agree to adjust your PAYE code to collect the tax  retrospectively in following tax years.

You will then pay tax either through self-assessment  or HMRC may agree to adjust your PAYE code to collect the tax  retrospectively in following tax years. If you complete a self- assessment form on an annual basis then you will need to take the  information from the P11D form above and enter the information  on your self-assessment income tax return. See ‘Statutory and Unpaid Leave’ section for examples of  scenarios where you may receive a P11D form. Please note that  in the event that you are required to fund Barclays for the cost of  your vehicle during any period (with an amount equal to the gross  sacrifice that would have been operated for that car during the  salary sacrifice period), any payments you make in this way are  contributions towards the private use of the car, which will reduce  the applicable tax due in the tax year in question.

Please note that  in the event that you are required to fund Barclays for the cost of  your vehicle during any period (with an amount equal to the gross  sacrifice that would have been operated for that car during the  salary sacrifice period), any payments you make in this way are  contributions towards the private use of the car, which will reduce  the applicable tax due in the tax year in question. Does the car salary sacrifice value affect  my pay related employment benefits  such as Pension? No, these are based on your reference salary (i.e. your salary before  the salary reduction). In the 1-50g/km of CO2 band, the ‘electric range figure’ determines the appropriate percentage.

In the 1-50g/km of CO2 band, the ‘electric range figure’ determines the appropriate percentage. The electric range figure is the  number of miles which is the equivalent of the number of kilometres specified in a European Community (EC) certificate of  conformity, an EC type – approval certificate of a UK approval certificate on the basis of which a car is registered, as being the  maximum distance for which the car can be driven in electric mode without recharging the battery. Company Car Tax rates for cars first registered after 6 April 2020 Emissions  (CO2 g/km) Electric range (miles) 2022/23 2023/24 2024/25 2025/26 2026/27 2027/28 0   2% 2% 2% 3% 4% 5% 1-50     Zero   emission  miles 130+ 2% 2% 2% 3% 4% 5% 70-129 5% 5% 5% 6% 7% 8% 40-69 8% 8% 8% 9% 10% 11% 30-39 12% 12% 12% 13% 14% 15% <30 14% 14% 14% 15% 16% 17%.

10      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Will the Scheme affect statutory payments  or state benefits? Statutory payments and state benefits that are based on  earnings subject to National Insurance may be affected by a  salary sacrifice arrangement. Statutory payments that may  be affected include statutory maternity pay and statutory sick  pay. State benefits that may be affected include the maternity  allowance or Universal Credit. Further guidance can also be found on the HMRC website and you  may wish to review this. You may also wish to seek independent  advice on your personal circumstances. Please note that  Barclays does not accept any responsibility for any impact to any  individual’s state provided benefits through joining the Scheme. Will the Scheme affect tax credits? Participating in the Scheme generally should not adversely affect  any tax credits you receive. Further guidance can also be found on  the HMRC website and you may wish to review this.

Further guidance can also be found on  the HMRC website and you may wish to review this. You may also  wish to seek independent advice on your personal circumstances. First payment The gross sacrifice amount of the vehicle will be deducted in  advance. Please be aware that if your vehicle is delivered after the  Barclays payroll cut-off date, or if Barclays are not notified before  this date, your following pay will receive a larger deduction to take  account of the period from the point of car delivery. Deductions  will then continue as expected from the next payroll. Salary sacrifice and tax Will I be given any formal advice on  the suitability of the Scheme for my  circumstances? Please be aware that information provided in this Driver Handbook  and on the Drive Electric website does not in any way constitute the  provision of financial advice by Barclays or Ayvens. You should seek  independent financial advice if you are unsure.

You should seek  independent financial advice if you are unsure. Reimbursement of business mileage   Colleagues must pay for fuel directly and reclaim the expense as out  of pocket expenses for business miles. The mileage reimbursement  rates paid by Barclays are the Advisory Electric Rate (AER) and  Advisory Fuel Rates (AFR) as published by HM Revenue & Customs  (HMRC). HMRC, as well as Barclays, may change these rates at any  time. These rates apply to company cars provided through Drive  Electric, and also to any replacement car which may be provided. https://www.gov.uk/guidance/advisory-fuel-rates Additionally, colleagues in Drive Electric vehicles who undertake  business miles will receive a 10 pence per mile Business  Mileage Allowance as a contribution towards wear and tear  costs incurred through business mileage.

https://www.gov.uk/guidance/advisory-fuel-rates Additionally, colleagues in Drive Electric vehicles who undertake  business miles will receive a 10 pence per mile Business  Mileage Allowance as a contribution towards wear and tear  costs incurred through business mileage. This Business Mileage  Allowance will be calculated using the miles claimed (and fully  processed) through expenses for miles driven in Drive Electric  vehicles, multiplied by the agreed pence per mile rate. Business  Mileage Allowance payments will be made through payroll  (subject to tax and NI) in the month following the expense claim..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    11 Who is eligible to participate in the Scheme? Eligibility to participate Drive Electric is at the sole discretion of  Barclays. To be eligible to participate in the Scheme you must: •	 Be employed by the Barclays Group and not be a member of  any other Barclays provided company car scheme. •	 Have sufficient salary to sacrifice at the date of joining and  when placing any order for a new car under the scheme.

•	 Have sufficient salary to sacrifice at the date of joining and  when placing any order for a new car under the scheme. Please be aware that you will not be permitted to sacrifice  an amount of salary which would take you below the NMW,  NLW, or into statutory pay (for example statutory maternity  pay or statutory sick pay) •	 Not have been placed at risk of redundancy or had  displacement confirmed, or otherwise be aware of a leave  date from your employment with Barclays at the point of  placing a new order •	 Be a UK taxpayer •	 Meet any conditions set by Ayvens, for example driving  licence validation For insurance eligibility purposes you and your additional driver  (now included as standard, but must be a partner or spouse  living at the same address) must at the date of the order request:  •	 Have a full UK or EU driving licence •	 Have no more than 9 driving licence penelty points  •	 Have no more than 2 own fault accidents in the past 3 years •	 Be aged 18-75 (please read the acceptance criteria for the  open driving policy as this is different and please note the  excess variable

Please be aware that you will not be permitted to sacrifice  an amount of salary which would take you below the NMW,  NLW, or into statutory pay (for example statutory maternity  pay or statutory sick pay) •	 Not have been placed at risk of redundancy or had  displacement confirmed, or otherwise be aware of a leave  date from your employment with Barclays at the point of  placing a new order •	 Be a UK taxpayer •	 Meet any conditions set by Ayvens, for example driving  licence validation For insurance eligibility purposes you and your additional driver  (now included as standard, but must be a partner or spouse  living at the same address) must at the date of the order request:  •	 Have a full UK or EU driving licence •	 Have no more than 9 driving licence penelty points  •	 Have no more than 2 own fault accidents in the past 3 years •	 Be aged 18-75 (please read the acceptance criteria for the  open driving policy as this is different and please note the  excess variable You are responsible for notifying Ayvens immediately via the  Driverline of any changes to circumstances which might affect  your eligibility.

You are responsible for notifying Ayvens immediately via the  Driverline of any changes to circumstances which might affect  your eligibility. Ayvens and Barclays reserve the right to change the eligibility  requirements from time to time, or to withdraw eligibility to  join the scheme if, for example, you fail to follow the guidelines  contained in this Driver Handbook. Who else is eligible to drive the car? As of the 11th August 2024, at the point of placing an order  in the drive electric website, an additional driver is included  as standard on your policy. This person must be a partner or  spouse living at the same address. If you are insured by AXA or  Allianz, unless you specified and added an additional driver,  only you are insured to drive the vehicle under your policy. As of 11th August 2024, at the point of placing an order,  you also have the option at that time only to tick the ‘Open  Driving’ option. This allows any driver over the age of 25 to drive your vehicle.

This allows any driver over the age of 25 to drive your vehicle. However, they must meet the insurance criteria:  •	 Have a full UK or EU driving licence •	 Have no more than 9 driving licence penalty points •	 Have no more than 2 fault accidents in the past 3 years •	 Be aged 25 - 75  The Open Driving option is at the Barclays employees discretion  and it is yours & your additional drivers responsibility to make  sure that you meet the insurance conditions. If any driver does not meet the conditions, their insurance will  be invalid. It is not possible for drivers to take out a seperate insurance  policy on the Drive Electric vehicle. How many cars can I take through  the Scheme? You are entitled to one salary sacrifice company car  through the Scheme. What if I am part-time? All employees on Barclays payroll are eligible to join this  Scheme providing they meet the eligibility criteria detailed  within this document. Eligibility and choice.

12      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Eligibility and choice Are there any restrictions on my car choice? The maximum CO2 limit for any vehicle within Drive Electric  is 50 grams/km. This is restricted within the Drive Electric  site. There are no exceptions to this restriction which is set  in recognition of the environmental impact of car travel and  in line with Barclays objective to reduce CO2 emissions per  kilometre of business car travel. What salary sacrifice periods are available? The standard car term is 36 months, but for added flexibility you  are also able to select a 24 month term, which could be more  expensive but will allow you to change your car more frequently,  or a 48 month term. The salary sacrifice period will match the  underlying term of your agreement and both will start when the  car is delivered to you. What mileage options are available to me? Mileage will be variable and dependent on your requirements.

What mileage options are available to me? Mileage will be variable and dependent on your requirements. Please consider both your business and private mileage  requirements through the course of the year. You can select  mileage from 6,000 total miles per annum up to 50,000 total  miles per annum in increments of 2,000 miles. What happens if I exceed the  mileage allowance? The Scheme allows you to select your expected annual mileage  and you must pay due care and attention to this point to ensure  as accurate an estimate as possible is provided. At the end of  the sacrifice period if the vehicle has more mileage than you  had selected then a mileage variation charge will apply, which  reflects the additional running costs/depreciation suffered  by Ayvens on that vehicle. This amount will be charged by  Ayvens to Barclays and Barclays will pass this cost on to you  via a deduction from net pay through payroll.

This amount will be charged by  Ayvens to Barclays and Barclays will pass this cost on to you  via a deduction from net pay through payroll. The most cost  efficient approach for you is to ensure all required mileage is  reflected in the original quote, such that the tax efficiency of  the salary sacrifice mechanism can apply to that cost. It would,  therefore, be best to slightly overestimate your mileage than  underestimate it. The charges may take up to 3 months to be processed after the  end of the sacrifice period depending on the payroll cut off. You  will be notified in writing in advance of the value of the charges,  which may be deducted from your regular salary or from any  other payments due to you by Barclays. In the event that it is not  possible to recoup all monies owed from your salary or other  payments due to you by Barclays, you will be required to arrange  an alternative method of payment.

In the event that it is not  possible to recoup all monies owed from your salary or other  payments due to you by Barclays, you will be required to arrange  an alternative method of payment. What happens if I drive fewer miles than my  selected allowance? If, at the end of the sacrifice period, you have overestimated your  mileage, you will receive a gross reimbursement from Barclays  through payroll. This reimbursement will be subject to income  tax and NIC as normal for cash paid through payroll. What happens if I have to return my  vehicle early? If you have to return your vehicle early and there is a  mileage variation (pro-rated at the point of return) there  would be no additional charge or reimbursement due  to you as this will be included in the Early Termination  Charge (see Early Termination section).

What happens if I have to return my  vehicle early? If you have to return your vehicle early and there is a  mileage variation (pro-rated at the point of return) there  would be no additional charge or reimbursement due  to you as this will be included in the Early Termination  Charge (see Early Termination section). What happens if I purchase my vehicle? If you choose to purchase your vehicle at the end of your  contract and there is a mileage variation, there will be an  additional charge or reimbursement due to you as this will not  be included in the Purchase Price of the car. This will be arranged  through payroll. However, if you terminate the vehicle contract early and would  like to purchase the vehicle, the mileage variation will be  included in the early termination calculation..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    13 Eligibility and choice Can I add accessories to the car at order? It is possible to add factory fit options to your chosen vehicle  and these must be selected using the Drive Electric website. A  wide range of options are available on each vehicle but these  are restricted to manufacturer factory fit choices only. When  selecting your accessories, please remember that the total of the  full VAT inclusive list price of the accessories is added to the list  price of the car to calculate the Benefit in Kind tax charge (see  section “How is the tax for the car calculated?”). Standard items will be available in the Drive Electric portal under  dealer fit accessories. If you are not able to find the item you are  looking for please call the Driverline for assistance. Can I select a tow bar? A tow bar can be specified as an accessory at the time of  ordering your vehicle.

Can I select a tow bar? A tow bar can be specified as an accessory at the time of  ordering your vehicle. This will become a taxable accessory and  its list price will be added to the list price of the car for taxation  purposes. However, if a tow bar is required after delivery of the  vehicle you will need to seek authorisation from Ayvens. There  are limits on the maximum gross trailer weight that can be  towed by each vehicle and not all EVs are able to have a tow bar  fitted. We would recommend seeking advice from the dealer who  supplied your vehicle before fitting a tow bar. If agreed all costs  will be met by yourself. Please note if a tow bar was not fitted as part of the original  order, tow bars and associated fixtures should be removed  prior to return of the vehicle. Any holes or damage should be  made good to a professional standard. You must ensure that  your driving licence permits you to drive the vehicle with the  trailer attached.

You must ensure that  your driving licence permits you to drive the vehicle with the  trailer attached. Drivers who passed their UK driving test after 1  January 1997 will have had to pass an additional test in order to  tow a trailer over 750kg. Note that this is the laden weight and  not just the weight of the trailer and careful consideration must  be made in regards to what is being towed. Failure to do so is an  offence in law and is subject to a fixed penalty notice and/or a  licence endorsement. Please note that if permission to tow is given you should obtain  additional insurance cover for your trailer or caravan. This must  be arranged through Ayvens Insurance. Can I use my personalised number plate? The use of personalised number plates is permitted on cars  through Drive Electric. Due to DVLA regulations, there are  certain procedures that must be followed in order to have a  personalised number plate assigned to a ‘Lease Company  Vehicle’.

Due to DVLA regulations, there are  certain procedures that must be followed in order to have a  personalised number plate assigned to a ‘Lease Company  Vehicle’. If you would like your personalised number plate  transferred to your vehicle, please advise Ayvens when you  receive your order confirmation, they can then confirm the  process for you. You will be required to pay Ayvens for any costs  associated with the transfer of personalised number plates  including removal at the end of the contract..

14      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Insurance What are the insurance arrangements for  Drive Electric? The vehicle will be insured on a fully comprehensive basis  throughout the salary sacrifice period. Ayvens is the policyholder  as the party who are the owners of the vehicles and have ultimate  responsibility to ensure that it is insured. Upon delivery you will  be provided with a certificate detailing your name only, but this  will cover an additional driver (partner or spouse living at the  same address) and if you have ticked the ‘Open Driving’ option,  any driver that meets the insurance criteria. Ayvens are providing you with a fully insured vehicle. You (and  any other named drivers) benefit from the policy just as you  would under your own motor insurance policy. You are also  bound by the policy terms and conditions. You will receive a  Certificate of Insurance, every year throughout the term of your  agreement.

You will receive a  Certificate of Insurance, every year throughout the term of your  agreement. The full insurance policy is held by Ayvens and you  can request a copy for your records. As this is a group insurance policy, claims will be handled by  Ayvens, so drivers should not contact the insurer directly. The price of the insurance will be individually rated, based  on a short series of questions asked when the vehicle quote  is provided through Drive Electric. You must provide accurate  and honest answers to all questions or the insurance may not  be valid, leaving you liable for any costs. Insurance costs are  factored into the gross salary sacrifice and are fixed for the  duration of the vehicle lease period and therefore the salary  sacrifice period. What is covered? •	 Fully comprehensive Class 1 insurance for you and a partner  or spouse living at the same address  •	 Business use for you and all other drivers if you are insured  under the Ayvens insurance policy.

What is covered? •	 Fully comprehensive Class 1 insurance for you and a partner  or spouse living at the same address  •	 Business use for you and all other drivers if you are insured  under the Ayvens insurance policy. If you are not insured  under the Ayvens insurance policy, only the Barclays  colleague is covered for Business use. •	 Uninsured Loss Recovery –  Where liability has been  accepted by a third party for a non-fault claim, recovery of  costs and expenses not covered by insurance, such as excess  payments. •	 All windscreen and glass replacement included up to the  service charge •	 Limited International Travel - limitations on overseas travel  are detailed in the Taking Your Vehicle Abroad section below Who can the insurance cover?  If your are covered under Ayvens Insurance, the insurance  scheme will allow: • 	 As standard, your partner or spouse living at the same  address as you.

•	 All windscreen and glass replacement included up to the  service charge •	 Limited International Travel - limitations on overseas travel  are detailed in the Taking Your Vehicle Abroad section below Who can the insurance cover?  If your are covered under Ayvens Insurance, the insurance  scheme will allow: • 	 As standard, your partner or spouse living at the same  address as you. • 	 Drivers with up to 9 driving licence penalty points  • 	 Drivers with up to 2 fault incidents in the past 3 years. Drivers that do not meet the insurance criteria set out by Ayvens  will not be eligible for cover and therefore insurance will be  refused. If you are insured under the previous insurer, AXA or Allianz, the  scheme will allow:  • 	 If selected at the time of order, a partner or spouse living at  the same address. • 	 Drivers with up to 7 driving licence penalty points  • 	 Drivers with up to 1 fault incidents in the past 3 years.

• 	 Drivers with up to 7 driving licence penalty points  • 	 Drivers with up to 1 fault incidents in the past 3 years. If you are unsure, please check your most recent insurance  certificate or call the Barclays Driverline..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    15 Insurance Are there any Excesses in the event  of a claim? In the event of an insurance claim, Ayvens, as the insured party,  is responsible for the Excess. This cost will be charged to you  in the form of a Service Charge, collected by the repairer on  completion of the repair. If you are due to leave Barclays whilst the vehicle is in for  repair, you must advise Ayvens, to ensure that the Service  Charge is collected. The majority of Barclays colleagues will be insured under the  Ayvens insurance. You will be able to tell by checking the last  insurance certificate you recieved, or by calling the Barclays  Driverline. If you are not under the Ayvens insurance, you will be  coverd by either AXA or Allianz. If you are insured by Ayvens, the following criteria applies. Accidental damage, fire and theft is £250 per claim, subject to  the following:  •	 £1,000 for drivers under 21 years of age. • 	 Windscreen claims £100.

• 	 Windscreen claims £100. • 	 £1,000 if vehicle performance is enhanced through Over Air  Update • 	 £50 replacement or repair to electric charging cable   The above amounts are payable regardless of fault of claim. If your insurance certificate specifies that AXA is your insurer, the  following criteria applies. Accidental damage, fire and theft is  £250 per claim, subject to the following: • 	 £500 for drivers aged 21- 24 and • 	 £425 for drivers aged 25 and over with a full UK licence for  less than 12 months at the time of loss or damage. • 	 Drivers aged under 21 will be on an individual basis during  the order process. • 	 Windscreen claims £100. If your car was ordered before 1 October 2021, your insurer  will be Allianz and different charges are applicable and can be  requested from the Driverline. The above amounts are payable regardless of fault of claim. No claims bonus Your no claims bonus will not accrue under this Scheme.

No claims bonus Your no claims bonus will not accrue under this Scheme. Ayvens  will be able to provide you with confirmation of your accident  history, which many insurers would take into account for future  motor insurance. Personal Property Loss of or damage to personal belongings is covered up to a limit  (defined by the insurer). Valuable items should not be left in your vehicle overnight and  should be hidden from view wherever possible..

16      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Insurance Do you check my driving licence? You will be requested to provide your consent for Ayvens (and/ or its agent) to undertake a driving licence validation [for you  only] by completing an online driving licence compliance check  mandate. Completion of a licence validation is a condition of the  insurance policy and it is not possible to deliver a vehicle without  the check being completed. Please ensure you read and understand the terms of the form  before completing and submitting the mandate. The information provided by the DVLA will only be used for  risk assessment, insurance and assessing the suitability of  individuals to drive a vehicle. All information will be treated in  the strictest of confidence. If the result of the licence check excludes you from joining/  continuing in the Scheme Ayvens will contact you.

If the result of the licence check excludes you from joining/  continuing in the Scheme Ayvens will contact you. Providing information to the Insurer All the information provided during the quote and order  process must be complete and correct as far as you know. You  are responsible for making sure that information relating to  all named drivers requested to be covered by the policy is also  complete and correct. If it is discovered that you (or someone  acting for you) deliberately gave incomplete or false information  in the insurance application, all cover under the policy will  end. This event will cause the early termination of your salary  sacrifice period and the company car will be returned to Ayvens. Barclays will deduct the full cost of the early termination  from your salary as a net deduction from payroll (see early  termination section). During the life of the vehicle your circumstances may change.

During the life of the vehicle your circumstances may change. It is your responsibility to advise Ayvens immediately of any  changes to circumstances which might affect insurance should  this occur during the salary sacrifice period, examples being if  you/your named drivers receive additional penalty points to now  exceed 9 points or if you lose your licence. In the event of a claim to the insurance company it is found that  you have provided inaccurate data or not maintained accurate  data that would affect or invalidate the insurance, (e.g. if you  lose your driving licence) you will be responsible for the full  uninsured damages associated with the accident as though this  was your own car and had no insurance. You are not insured under this policy to drive any other vehicle..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    17 Early terminations What happens if I have to leave the  Scheme early? When you join the Scheme you are committing to take the  company car for the full salary sacrifice period and you should  not join if you are unable to do this. If your employment with Barclays terminates for any reason  during the salary sacrifice period, or if you fail to continue to  meet the eligibility criteria (e.g. because you are seconded  overseas and are no longer a UK taxpayer, or because you lose  your driving licence) then you will be required to return the car  to Barclays on such date as is agreed between you and Barclays  or as soon as reasonably practicable. Any failure to return the car promptly will be treated as a  disciplinary matter and you may incur financial penalties  (up to the full value of the car) which will in the first instance  be collected via deduction from your net salary or any other  sums due to you from Barclays.

Any failure to return the car promptly will be treated as a  disciplinary matter and you may incur financial penalties  (up to the full value of the car) which will in the first instance  be collected via deduction from your net salary or any other  sums due to you from Barclays. In joining the Scheme, you are  expressly authorising Barclays to make any such deductions. If circumstances arise that necessitate the early termination of  your salary sacrifice arrangement, Barclays may incur an early  termination charge from Ayvens for the early termination and,  depending on the nature and timing of the circumstances, you  may be required to reimburse Barclays via a deduction from your  net salary or other payments due to you through payroll. In the event that it is not possible to recoup all monies owed  from your net final salary or any other payments due to you, you  will be required to arrange an alternative method of payment.

In the event that it is not possible to recoup all monies owed  from your net final salary or any other payments due to you, you  will be required to arrange an alternative method of payment. Any early termination charge payable by you would be capped  at a level equal to 3 times the gross monthly salary sacrifice  value. It will be collected from you via a deduction from your net  salary or other sums due to you through payroll. Circumstances  (arising at any time during the salary sacrifice period) that will  normally give rise to an early termination charge payable by you  are as follows: •	 Dismissal (by any other reason than redundancy or ill-  health) •	 Loss of licence due to driver disqualification (and therefore  enforced/requested return of the car) •	 any circumstances not outlined below (e.g. a personal  request to return the car early) Please note you will be unable to rejoin the Scheme again until  your original lease period has ended.

a personal  request to return the car early) Please note you will be unable to rejoin the Scheme again until  your original lease period has ended. Circumstances (arising at any time during the salary sacrifice  period) that will normally not give rise to an early termination  charge payable by you (subject to management discretion) are  as follows: •	 Redundancy •	 Termination of employment due to ill-health •	 Loss of driving licence on medical grounds •	 Death Other circumstances will give rise to an early termination charge  payable by you if they occur during the first 6 months of the  salary sacrifice period (following delivery of your Ayvens car), but  will not normally give rise to an early termination charge payable  by you (subject to management discretion), if they occur after the  first 6 months: •	 Resignation •	 Permanent injury •	 Non-return from extended parental leave (not normal  maternity, paternity, adoption leave).

18      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Periods of leave Tax The vehicle will continue to be a taxable benefit for the duration  of the salary sacrifice period, irrespective of leave type and tax  will be collected accordingly. Please see ‘How will I be taxed on  the car benefit?’ section for further information of the benefit  reporting process in this scenario. Maternity/Paternity/Adoption Leave  (“Statutory Leave”) You will retain the use of the vehicle during any period of  Statutory Leave and the salary sacrifice agreement will remain  in place, though your earnings will not fall below any statutory  minimum pay levels. If the salary sacrifice period expires during your Statutory  Leave you will be able to order a new vehicle via Drive Electric. However, if you do not intend to return to Barclays at the end of  your Statutory Leave, an Early Termination charge may apply to  your new vehicle.

However, if you do not intend to return to Barclays at the end of  your Statutory Leave, an Early Termination charge may apply to  your new vehicle. Instead, you may wish to consider extending  the term of your current vehicle. Long Term Sickness You will retain the use of the vehicle during the period of  sickness and the salary sacrifice agreement will remain in place,  though your earnings will not fall below any statutory minimum  pay levels. If the salary sacrifice period expires during your long term  sickness, you will be required to return the vehicle. You will be  able to order a new vehicle if you can meet the terms of the  salary sacrifice agreement. If you are unable to enter into a  salary sacrifice agreement at the point when an order for a new  car would be placed, you will be unable to order a new car until  you return to normal employment (unless Barclays decides  otherwise at its sole discretion). Other types of leave (e.g.

Other types of leave (e.g. career break) Provided that your employment with Barclays is continuing, you  will not be required to return the vehicle during the period of  unpaid leave/reduction in salary. If the salary sacrifice period expires during your leave, then you  will be required to return the vehicle. You will be able to order  a new vehicle if you can meet the terms of the salary sacrifice  agreement. If you are unable to enter into a salary sacrifice  agreement at the point when an order for a new car would be  placed, you will be unable to order a new car until you return to  normal employment If you are on leave which results in zero pay, or pay is at a level  which would result in a partial sacrifice down to statutory  levels (considering all benefits you have selected), then you are  required to fund the shortfall to Barclays. You will be required to  pay an amount equal to the gross sacrifice that cannot be made  in any one pay period during the leave.

You will be required to  pay an amount equal to the gross sacrifice that cannot be made  in any one pay period during the leave. For example, if the gross sacrifice value is £400 per month and  you are in a period of unpaid leave, then you are required to  fund £400. Similarly, if you reduce your hours (and therefore pay)  which results in only £300 being sacrificed (i.e. a partial sacrifice  down to NMW), then you are required to fund the remaining £100. Please note that any payments you make in this way will reduce the  applicable tax in the relevant tax year..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    19 Looking after your car 28 day relief vehicle To ensure continued short-term mobility, the Drive Electric  scheme includes a relief vehicle if your vehicle is off the road for  mechanical reasons or if you have been involved in an accident  and are going through the accident management process. The relief vehicle is available to you for a maximum of 28 days  per incident and this period cannot be extended under any  scenario, even if your vehicle is still off the road due to delays at  the garage/repair agent or insurer. Whilst your vehicle is off the  road, we will try to arrange a courtesy vehicle with the relevant  garage/ repair agent or manufacturer, however, we cannot offer  any guarantee that a vehicle would be available.

Whilst your vehicle is off the  road, we will try to arrange a courtesy vehicle with the relevant  garage/ repair agent or manufacturer, however, we cannot offer  any guarantee that a vehicle would be available. Ayvens will work with the garage/repair agent or insurer to  reduce the time your vehicle is off the road as much as possible  however, if your vehicle is off the road for over 28 days and a  courtesy vehicle is not available, then you will need to arrange  alternative mobility. The salary sacrifice agreement is required to continue whilst  your vehicle is being repaired. Driver Neglect If the vehicle sustains damage due to the driver misuse and abuse  or the driver neglecting to ensure that the vehicle is properly  serviced and maintained, the driver shall be responsible for the  cost of restoring the vehicle to a properly serviceable condition. This can affect the manufacturer’s warranty and may void any  warranty liability for parts in the future.

This can affect the manufacturer’s warranty and may void any  warranty liability for parts in the future. You will also be charged  for driver neglect of the vehicle under the following scenarios: •	 Damage (not reported to Accident Management) •	 Lost items (including keys and service manuals) •	 Incorrect fuel (diesel in a petrol engine and vice versa) •	 Failure to top up oil leading to the engine seizing •	 Malicious tyre damage •	 Lost wheel locking nuts Duty of care You are personally responsible for the roadworthiness of the  vehicle in accordance with legal requirements as if you were  the owner of the vehicle. Ayvens determines lease costs on the  assumption that the vehicle will be kept in good condition. It is therefore in the interest of all parties that the vehicle is  maintained and kept in good condition and that the vehicle is as  safely and securely parked or stored as is possible.

It is therefore in the interest of all parties that the vehicle is  maintained and kept in good condition and that the vehicle is as  safely and securely parked or stored as is possible. You are under  a duty to report all damage and defects immediately to Ayvens  and to comply with the Terms and Conditions of the Scheme. Servicing and Maintenance You are responsible for keeping the vehicle in a clean and  satisfactory condition. In order for the vehicle to be safe and  perform well, Ayvens recommends that you carry out regular  maintenance checks in-line with the manufacturer’s handbook. For safety reasons and to ensure the vehicle is maintained in the  best condition, the vehicle must be serviced and maintained, at  a garage approved by Ayvens. The costs of all normal servicing  and repair are included in the cost of the vehicle and should be  arranged via the Driverline. Depending on the location of the  garage you may benefit from a collection and delivery service..

20      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Looking after your car   Safety Recalls From time to time manufacturers will issue safety recall notices. Ayvens will receive safety recalls and forward these to drivers. Upon receipt of a safety recall notice you should contact the  Driverline to arrange for the vehicle to attend a franchised dealer  as soon as possible. Ayvens will continue to issue safety recall  reminders until confirmation has been received that the required  work has been carried out. If you receive a recall notice, it is your  responsibility to ensure that you book an appointment for the  safety work to take place. Breakdown Assistance and Recovery You have access to 5-star emergency cover, 365 days a year, 24  hours a day, in Europe. Should roadside assistance be required,  please call the Ayvens Driverline. Glass and Windscreens Many car windscreens can be repaired, including small stone  chips which are out of the driver’s line of sight.

Glass and Windscreens Many car windscreens can be repaired, including small stone  chips which are out of the driver’s line of sight. In the event of  screen damage contact Ayvens. The costs of replacement and  repair of vehicle glass is included in the leasing agreement  although there will be a service charge (see insurance section). Tyres The costs of replacement tyres are included. It is the  responsibility of the driver to ensure that the vehicle always  has at least the minimum legal tread depth around the  circumference and width of the tyres. Replacement tyres can be  arranged via the Driverline. The legal minimum requirement for tread depth is currently  1.6mm of tread across the central three-quarters of the tyre. Ayvens will replace tyres at 2mm. Tyres with bald patches, bulges  or cuts over 2.5mm long exposing the cords are also illegal and  unsafe. It is your responsibility to check your tyres regularly to  ensure compliance.

It is your responsibility to check your tyres regularly to  ensure compliance. Failure to comply with this requirement can  lead to a fine and three penalty points for each defective tyre. Recurring Technical Faults If your vehicle suffers from a recurring technical problem,  contact the Driverline. The problem will be escalated with  the dealer and manufacturer and you will be kept advised on  progress of until a satisfactory resolution is agreed. Cleanliness It is the driver’s responsibility to keep the vehicle clean and tidy  both inside and out. Taxing your Vehicle (Road Fund Licence) Vehicle tax is included in the cost of the vehicle and will be  arranged by Ayvens. MOT It is your responsibility to ensure the vehicle has a valid MOT. Failure to have a valid MOT invalidates insurance cover and can  cause delays in re-taxing the vehicle. MOT reminders will be issued to you in advance of the expiry  date to allow you time to book an MOT for the vehicle via the  Ayvens Driverline..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    21 Looking after your car Fines and Fixed Penalty Notices Other than any fines that have been left on your vehicle by the  charging authority, Ayvens as the registered keeper will receive  your fine. Any fine received, will attract an Administration fee for the  processing of the fine that will be recovered via payroll. You will be able to dispute a fine directly with the issuing  authority. If a fine is disputed and successfully overturned,  please send the proof to Ayvens and we will reimburse the  administration fee which will be processed via payroll. Wherever possible liability will be transferred to you, as the  named driver. You will then receive a communication directly  from the charging authority. A communication relating to the  handling of your fine will be emailed to you along with advice on  how to dispute the fine should you wish to do so.

A communication relating to the  handling of your fine will be emailed to you along with advice on  how to dispute the fine should you wish to do so. Any fines not paid within the stated period will be paid by Ayvens  and the cost forwarded to Barclays. Barclays will recover the full  cost of the fine  via payroll. In placing an order for a company car under this scheme, you  are expressly authorising Barclays to make any necessary  deductions for fines and sundry charges as described in this  Driver Handbook. See below for more details on the types of fines and the process  for each. Speeding Fines and Toll Charges - As the registered keeper of  the vehicle Ayvens will receive speeding fine notifications. Upon  receipt Ayvens will supply driver details to the issuing authority. It is ultimately your responsibility to pay any speeding fines  incurred.

It is ultimately your responsibility to pay any speeding fines  incurred. Fines for using mobile phones - If you use a mobile phone whilst  driving and are caught it is your responsibility to pay any fines  associated. Parking Fines  It is your responsibility to pay parking fines. As the registered  keeper of the vehicle Ayvens will receive outstanding parking fine  notifications. Upon receipt Ayvens will supply driver’s details to  the issuing authority. The fine should be disputed as soon as the fine notification and  dispute process communication is received from Ayvens. Failure  to dispute in a timely manner may result in Ayvens having to  make payment on your behalf and this will be recharged in full. If Ayvens receive a final demand, they are unable to withhold  payment. Any fines not paid within the stated period will be  recharged to Barclays via Ayvens. Barclays will recover the cost  of the fine via payroll.

Barclays will recover the cost  of the fine via payroll. Bus Lane  As the registered keeper of the vehicle Ayvens will receive  notification of bus lane and toll fines. Ayvens is unable to refuse payment on the grounds of the  vehicle being a lease car. Therefore, payments will be made and  recharged to Barclays. Ayvens will communicate the receipt of  the Bus Lane to you via email. The fine will be recharged to you  in full, via payroll. Congestion Charges and Clean Air Zone  (CAZ) Fines  As the registered keeper of the vehicle Ayvens will receive  notification of any congestion charge or CAZ fines. Ayvens is unable to refuse payment on the grounds of the  vehicle being a lease car. Therefore, payments will be made and  recharged to Barclays. Ayvens will communicate the receipt of  the congestion charge or CAZ fine to you via email. The fine will  be recharged to you in full, via payroll..

22      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Accident Management What is provided? A full accident management service is provided as part of the  scheme including: •	 Claims management •	 Repair management •	 Like for like (where possible) relief vehicle for up to 28 days Ayvens preferred suppliers must be used for all accident repairs. If you take your vehicle to a non-approved repairer you will be  recharged any additional costs over and above Ayvens’ agreed  rates, plus an administration fee.

If you take your vehicle to a non-approved repairer you will be  recharged any additional costs over and above Ayvens’ agreed  rates, plus an administration fee. Immediate action at the scene If an accident does occur you must always act to safeguard the  interests of yourself, Barclays and the Insurers as follows: •	 Never admit liability (this includes saying “sorry”) •	 Do not negotiate to settle any claim •	 Take full details of all vehicles involved, together with drivers’  names and addresses, their insurers (if available) and verify  identification if possible (by asking for evidence of identity) •	 Sketch out a rough plan of the accident scene, with road  names and numbers, junctions, time and place of accident  etc as soon as possible (only if it is safe to do so) •	 Obtain full details of any witnesses involved – independent  or otherwise •	 Give your name and address, company name and address  and insurer (as detailed on your cover note) to all third  parties •	 Inform the police if anyone involved is injured or there is  damage to property •	 The police must also be informed in cases of theft or if a  third party did n

Immediate action at the scene If an accident does occur you must always act to safeguard the  interests of yourself, Barclays and the Insurers as follows: •	 Never admit liability (this includes saying “sorry”) •	 Do not negotiate to settle any claim •	 Take full details of all vehicles involved, together with drivers’  names and addresses, their insurers (if available) and verify  identification if possible (by asking for evidence of identity) •	 Sketch out a rough plan of the accident scene, with road  names and numbers, junctions, time and place of accident  etc as soon as possible (only if it is safe to do so) •	 Obtain full details of any witnesses involved – independent  or otherwise •	 Give your name and address, company name and address  and insurer (as detailed on your cover note) to all third  parties •	 Inform the police if anyone involved is injured or there is  damage to property •	 The police must also be informed in cases of theft or if a  third party did n The policy will provide you with a crime  number for future reference.

The policy will provide you with a crime  number for future reference. You must then contact the Accident  Management Team without delay. Follow up actions In the event of an accident you should contact the Driverline  and the Accident Management team will manage the claim and  damage repair of the vehicle  The Accident Management team will take the following details of  the accident from you and notify the insurer: •	 Your registration number •	 Date and time of incident •	 Incident location •	 Damaged area of your vehicle •	 Home address •	 Third Party details, if applicable Once the information has been provided the team will arrange  for recovery or repairs to your vehicle. The team will advise you  of your reference number and provide you with access to track  your claim online. In certain circumstances you may be contacted at a later date to  provide additional information of the incident.

In certain circumstances you may be contacted at a later date to  provide additional information of the incident. If the incident involved a third party, regardless of fault, you  may receive correspondence from their insurance company. This is common practice, however, this should be forwarded  unanswered to the Accident Management team who will respond  on your behalf. If you have any queries, please contact the  Driverline. As in the case of any insurance claim, you will be required to pay  a Service Charge to Ayvens (see Insurance section)..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    23 Accident Management Non-driveable vehicles Should your vehicle be non-drivable, it will be recovered to  a garage straight away. At the time of your call alternative  transport will be arranged for you. The appointed garage will estimate the vehicle repair cost and  send the estimate through to Ayvens’ in-house engineers for  approval. Once approved (usually same day) the repairs will  commence and you will be kept updated on progress. If circumstances dictate it may be more cost-effective to repair  a vehicle near where the accident happened and transport the  repaired vehicle back to your home or office address. You will be given regular updates on repair progress. Write-off process If your vehicle is declared a total loss following an accident or  theft, you will still benefit from a relief vehicle for up to 28 days,  however, you will need to arrange alternative provision at the  end of this period.

Write-off process If your vehicle is declared a total loss following an accident or  theft, you will still benefit from a relief vehicle for up to 28 days,  however, you will need to arrange alternative provision at the  end of this period. Your salary sacrifice agreement, and salary reduction, will  continue until the vehicle is declared a total loss by the insurer. Once declared a total loss by the insurer, the salary sacrifice  agreement will be terminated from the incident date and any  overpayments will be refunded to you via payroll. In some circumstances, you may be able to order a new vehicle,  before the insurer’s declaration, if a total loss is expected  Driveable vehicles At the time of your call the Accident Management team will  allocate the repair to the closest approved bodyshop to your  home or work address – whichever is easier for you.

In some circumstances, you may be able to order a new vehicle,  before the insurer’s declaration, if a total loss is expected  Driveable vehicles At the time of your call the Accident Management team will  allocate the repair to the closest approved bodyshop to your  home or work address – whichever is easier for you. You will be given their details and asked to agree a suitable date  for a mobile estimate in the following seven days from reporting  the incident. This means that the garage will come out to your  vehicle either at your home or work address and note the  damage and take photos. The appointed garage will estimate the vehicle repair cost and  send the estimate through to Ayvens’ in-house engineers for  approval. Once approved (usually same day) the garage will  contact you to arrange a convenient date to collect and repair  your vehicle. You will be given regular updates on repair progress.

You will be given regular updates on repair progress. Will I receive a relief vehicle? You are eligible for a replacement for up to 28 days after the  vehicle is declared off the road. You will be provided with a  vehicle of a similar size to the vehicle you are driving, and we  will aim to provide either an electric or hybrid vehicle, where  available and appropriate. The salary sacrifice agreement will continue whilst your vehicle  is being repaired. Further information on the 28 day relief vehicle can be found on  page 19..

24      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Health and safety Safe Driving Programme  Barclays is committed to ensuring the health, safety and welfare  of its employees and, as far as is reasonably practicable, to  providing and maintaining safe working conditions. All colleagues driving on Barclays business are required to  adhere to the requirements of the Driving for Work Programme  and to complete and maintain a Driving for Work profile to be  permitted to drive for work. To complete the Driving for Work Programme or to check your  profile status, search ‘Driving for Work’ on the Barclays intranet  where you find a list of permitted drivers and links to your profile  and other useful information. Driver Fatigue Avoid driving when you are tired. Plan your journey to ensure  you are able to take regular breaks and if you have a long journey  consider splitting the journey by taking an overnight stay.

Plan your journey to ensure  you are able to take regular breaks and if you have a long journey  consider splitting the journey by taking an overnight stay. The early hours of the morning and early afternoon are when  drivers are most likely to feel tired. Only drive in the early hours if  you have to and if you are alert. You should take breaks every two hours for at least 15 minutes. Good Practice All drivers must report all incidents no matter how minor to  Accident Management. All drivers are responsible for ensuring the vehicle is safe to  drive, for example: •	 Do not overload the car •	 Do not allow the car to cause an obstruction •	 Do not reverse a car for long distances •	 Switch off the engine when stationary (except at traffic stops) •	 Use dipped headlights at night in built up areas and in dull  daytime weather, or if visibility is poor •	 Only use rear fog-lamps when visibility is poor, e.g.

All drivers are responsible for ensuring the vehicle is safe to  drive, for example: •	 Do not overload the car •	 Do not allow the car to cause an obstruction •	 Do not reverse a car for long distances •	 Switch off the engine when stationary (except at traffic stops) •	 Use dipped headlights at night in built up areas and in dull  daytime weather, or if visibility is poor •	 Only use rear fog-lamps when visibility is poor, e.g. in  extremely heavy rain or fog, ensuring they are turned off  when visibility improves (it is an offence to use them in good  visibility conditions) •	 Use lights half an hour before sunset and half an hour after  sunrise Mobile Phones Do not use mobile phones whilst driving. This is illegal and  expressly forbidden. Smoking Policy You must not smoke in your vehicle at any time, or allow anyone  else to do so. Health Ensure you have regular health and eyesight checks.
Health Ensure you have regular health and eyesight checks. Do not drive  if you are taking medication that carries a warning not to operate  machinery and do not allow anyone else to..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    25 Taking your vehicle abroad Foreign Travel Drivers will be permitted to take their vehicle abroad following  authorisation and receiving the appropriate documentation  from Ayvens. In order to arrange this please call the Driverline  and choose the option for Foreign travel. As this is a leased vehicle, you must obtain an overseas travel  pack, irrespective of your country of destination. This pack  contains your permission to travel documentation. There is a  cost for annual cover, which you will be required to pay to Ayvens  at the point of requesting cover. This will also provide key information of any legal requirements  relating to travel in each country. It will also contain key contact  information in case of breakdown or emergency overseas. These documents will be provided by Ayvens and you must  allow 14 days to process the documentation prior to travel. This  authorisation is valid for 12 months from receipt.

This  authorisation is valid for 12 months from receipt. Insurance Your vehicle is insured to drive in the UK and in any country  within the European Union. If you wish to drive outside the EU,  it is your responsibility to contact Ayvens via the Driverline and  they will arrange cover with you directly; you will be required to  pay for any additional premium. You will receive an email copy of your updated insurance  certificate following approval from Ayvens. Before you Travel Abroad Before you travel abroad you should carry out some basic checks  on the vehicle, as it can be difficult to arrange maintenance  repairs abroad, so it’s worthwhile ensuring that any urgent  maintenance work is completed in the UK prior to travel.

Before you Travel Abroad Before you travel abroad you should carry out some basic checks  on the vehicle, as it can be difficult to arrange maintenance  repairs abroad, so it’s worthwhile ensuring that any urgent  maintenance work is completed in the UK prior to travel. You should at least check the following: •	 Engine oil levels •	 Water coolant levels •	 Tyre pressures and tread depths •	 Wiper blades •	 Lights •	 Horn •	 Valid insurance certificate/foreign travel documentation •	 All legal requirements to drive in that country (e.g. high  visibility jackets, breathalysers, light diffusers) Accident If you are involved in an accident whilst travelling abroad then  you must report it in the same way as when travelling in the UK,  via the Driverline. Breakdown Should you breakdown overseas, you are covered by Ayvens’  European Breakdown cover and you can use the contact number  provided on the overseas travel documentation.

Breakdown Should you breakdown overseas, you are covered by Ayvens’  European Breakdown cover and you can use the contact number  provided on the overseas travel documentation. If any repairs to your Ayvens vehicle cannot be completed in  time for your return home then help is available. Details on  repatriation and onward travel can be found below..

26      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 Taking your vehicle abroad What is covered The full terms and conditions are supplied in the travel  documentation which must be requested prior to any European  travel including: •	 The cost of unaccompanied recovery for your vehicle, to a  single destination being either a) the vehicle’s permanent  home address or b) The nominated vehicle repairer in the  UK. This is conditional on Ayvens arranging recovery or  agreeing to the method of recovery in advance •	 You will also be covered for any reasonable storage charges  incurred in recovery providing you/your driver have made  every reasonable attempt to organise for your Vehicle to be  returned to the UK •	 Ayvens may, at their discretion, and depending on  circumstances, arrange and agree with you an alternative  method of recovered and cover other reasonable costs. What is not covered The limits of the repatriation and onward travel cover are  summarised below.

What is not covered The limits of the repatriation and onward travel cover are  summarised below. Please seek authorisation from Ayvens prior  to authorising any action to ensure cover is available. •	 Recovery where your vehicle only needs minor or  inexpensive repairs •	 If you choose to have your vehicle recovered onward to  the original destination there will be no entitlement to any  further recovery assistance for the full duration of that trip. •	 Separate transportation costs for personal effects, goods,  vehicles, caravans, boats or other waterborne craft carried in  or on your vehicle/trailer. These remain your responsibility  at all times. •	 Any additional costs incurred for your pets..

Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3    27 Ordering a replacement vehicle You will be able to order a new vehicle six months in advance of  the end of your salary sacrifice period. Please note new vehicles can take several months to be built and  can be impacted by global factors such as raw material shortages  and shipping delays. The Drive Electric portal provides access  to indicative lead times provided by manufacturers so please  review this when considering your order. Ayvens will work with the manufacturer of your choice to align  the delivery of your new vehicle with the contract end of your  current vehicle. The earlier you order your replacement vehicle  the greater the opportunity to align with your existing contract  end date. Please be aware that this may not always be possible  depending on the lead time of your chosen vehicle, in which case  Ayvens will work with the manufacturer to deliver your vehicle as  soon as is reasonably possible.

Please be aware that this may not always be possible  depending on the lead time of your chosen vehicle, in which case  Ayvens will work with the manufacturer to deliver your vehicle as  soon as is reasonably possible. If Ayvens have not received your replacement order 1 month  prior to your lease end date, you will be contacted and  arrangements will be made to collect your vehicle at the end of  the contract. Return of vehicles Vehicles should be clean inside and out to enable an accurate  estimation of any damage by the collection agent. Vehicles  should be returned with the following items: •	 Full set of keys (including any spares) •	 Stamped Service documentation (including service book  and instruction manuals) •	 Locking wheel nuts •	 Any other items received with the vehicle Any personal items should be removed from the vehicle,  including any security passes and window stickers as it may not  be possible to retrieve these at a later stage.

Vehicles  should be returned with the following items: •	 Full set of keys (including any spares) •	 Stamped Service documentation (including service book  and instruction manuals) •	 Locking wheel nuts •	 Any other items received with the vehicle Any personal items should be removed from the vehicle,  including any security passes and window stickers as it may not  be possible to retrieve these at a later stage. Vehicle collection If you are leaving Barclays, you will need to return your vehicle to  Ayvens. Failure to return the vehicle in a timely manner, will result  in the vehicle not being roadworthy and safe to drive. It is the  responsibility of the Barclays colleague and Ayvens to ensure the  vehicle is returned ahead of the colleague leave date.

It is the  responsibility of the Barclays colleague and Ayvens to ensure the  vehicle is returned ahead of the colleague leave date. End of period Can I purchase the vehicle? You may have an opportunity (but not the right) to purchase  the car at fair market value from Ayvens at the end of the salary  sacrifice agreement, providing a request to do so is made one  month before the end of the lease. Purchasing the car does not  prevent you from joining the Scheme again for a new car. Inspection of vehicle (a)	 Key for Key Exchange When returning a vehicle as a Key for Key Exchange (when the  dealer delivering a new vehicle also collects the return vehicle),  the delivery driver will complete a handover with you. Some  areas of damage may be recorded at this time. You are required  to sign the handover and are advised to retain a copy of this  document.

You are required  to sign the handover and are advised to retain a copy of this  document. A full BVRLA (British Vehicle Rental and Leasing  Association) inspection will be carried out on the vehicle when  it arrives at the Ayvens de-fleet Centre. The full inspection will  form the basis of any end of contract damage charges. (b)	 Collection of vehicle where no new vehicle is being delivered When the vehicle is collected and it is not a Key for Key Exchange,  the collection agent will complete a full BVRLA inspection with  you and you will be asked to sign to confirm the return condition  of the vehicle. This inspection will form the basis of any end of  contract damage charges. Inspections are conducted by a BVRLA accredited inspector,  in strict adherence with the BVRLA Standard of Fair Wear  and Tear, which is the industry standard and not exclusive to  Ayvens or Barclays..

28      Drive Electric – The Barclays Car Leasing Scheme  v2.3 End of period What happens at the end of the period in  the event of damage? You will not be charged for minor wear and tear to the vehicle  during the salary sacrifice period. You can expect to incur  charges if a vehicle is returned with an unreasonable level of  wear and tear or with items missing.

You can expect to incur  charges if a vehicle is returned with an unreasonable level of  wear and tear or with items missing. What is considered as  “unreasonable” is set out by the BVRLA and the main causes of  unreasonable wear and tear are: •	 Lack of regular checks by the vehicle user leading to faults  and damage going undetected and unrepaired •	 Not adhering to the vehicle manufacturer’s recommended  maintenance and servicing schedule •	 Drivers not taking responsibility for the day-to-day care and  maintenance of the vehicle leading to general neglect •	 Poor quality of body repairs (you should always use a Ayvens  approved repairer) •	 Missing stamps on service books/missing service books •	 Missing spare key Any charges will be forwarded to Barclays and Barclays will  pass this cost on to you via a deduction from net pay through  payroll.

What is considered as  “unreasonable” is set out by the BVRLA and the main causes of  unreasonable wear and tear are: •	 Lack of regular checks by the vehicle user leading to faults  and damage going undetected and unrepaired •	 Not adhering to the vehicle manufacturer’s recommended  maintenance and servicing schedule •	 Drivers not taking responsibility for the day-to-day care and  maintenance of the vehicle leading to general neglect •	 Poor quality of body repairs (you should always use a Ayvens  approved repairer) •	 Missing stamps on service books/missing service books •	 Missing spare key Any charges will be forwarded to Barclays and Barclays will  pass this cost on to you via a deduction from net pay through  payroll. In placing an order for a company car provided under  this scheme, you are expressly authorising Barclays to make any  such deductions via payroll if required.

In placing an order for a company car provided under  this scheme, you are expressly authorising Barclays to make any  such deductions via payroll if required. In the event that it is not possible to recoup all monies owed  from your salary, you will be required to arrange an alternative  method of payment. Are there any other charges? In placing an order under this Scheme, you are expressly  authorising Barclays to make any necessary deductions for any  charges outlined in this driver handbook. In summary these include, but are not limited to: •	 Cancellation charges – costs associated with cancelling a  vehicle order prior to delivery. •	 Early termination charges – any charges incurred for  terminating the vehicle before the agreed end date. •	 End of contract damage – any charges incurred for damage  considered “unreasonable”.

•	 End of contract damage – any charges incurred for damage  considered “unreasonable”. •	 Excess mileage charges – stated as pence per mile plus VAT,  will be charged at the end of the contract if the vehicle has  exceeded the total contracted mileage. •	 Fines – where these are met by either Ayvens or Barclays on  your behalf. •	 Driver neglect or unreported damage – including damaged  or broken items..
